text:
## 1. One node type: `channel`. "Project" becomes an attribute, not a concept.

A channel optionally carries a **workspace binding** (`machine + path`, i.e. what today makes something a project). One optional field on the node — explicitly *not* a subtype, not a different table, not a different code path. The word "project" survives only in human-facing rendering: a workspace-bearing channel gets shown as a project root. Everything in the schema, resolver, and daemon speaks only `channel`.

This matches what your schema already wants — you have one resolver (`channel_resolve.rs`), identity is `(parent, name)`, and the noun files already say "a channel and a project are the same thing." You're not redesigning; you're deleting the last place the duality leaks.
